## The eDNA Metabarcoding Workflow

![](assets/ednaworkflow.png){fig-align="center" width="80%"}


::: {style="font-size:0.6em; color:#5A8FA8; text-align:center; margin-top:8px;"}
*Figure adapted from NatureMetrics and Gill et al. 2016 · Emilie Boulanger*
:::

::: notes
The metabarcoding workflow has six main stages. 1 — Sample collection. 2 — DNA extraction and PCR amplification with specific primers. 3 — High-throughput sequencing generating millions of reads simultaneously. 4 — Bioinformatic treatment: quality filtering and OTU/ASV clustering. 5 — Taxonomic assignment against reference databases. 6 — Ecological analyses producing a presence/absence or abundance matrix across samples.
:::
